---
title: "Build Instructions"
lang: en
format: html
bibliography: references.bib
---

::: {.callout-note}
## 🇯🇵 日本語版 / Available in Japanese

日本語で読みたい方は [こちらのページ](build_ja.qmd) をご覧ください。  
Please see the [Japanese version](build_ja.qmd) for reading in Japanese.
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
This page is intended for developers.
:::

This section explains how to build the application.

The application is built using [PyInstaller](https://pyinstaller.org/en/stable/).

A pre-generated spec file is provided and should be used for building the application.  
Please note that this process can take some time (approximately 10 minutes).

```bash
pyinstaller build.spec
```
For more details on PyInstaller usage, please refer to the [official documentation](https://pyinstaller.org/en/stable/).

::: {.callout-caution}
## Notes

- Currently, builds have only been tested on **Windows**.  
  We plan to support other operating systems in the future.
- The provided spec file runs without errors, but there is still room for **refactoring** and **optimization**.
  Improvements will be made in future updates.
- For details about spec files, see [Using Spec FIles - PyInstaller](https://pyinstaller.org/en/stable/spec-files.html).
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Removing Previous Builds

Before running PyInstaller, it is recommended to **remove any existing build artifacts**:

```bash
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force build, dist
```

:::

## After Building

If the build completes successfully, the following directory structure will be generated at the project root:

```text
project/
├─ build/
└─ dist/
   └─ LeafShapeTool/
      ├─ LeafShapeTool.exe
      └─ _internal/
         └─ (library files)
```

The main executable is located at:

```bash
dist/LeafShapeTool/LeafShapeTool.exe
```

You can launch the application either by **double-clicking** the file or from the **command line**:

```bash
cd dist/LeafShapeTool/
LeafShapeTool.exe
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Double-Click vs. Command Line Launch

When launched by double-clicking, a console window appears for log and error output.
However, **the console closes automatically after execution ends**, which may make it difficult to check messages.
In such cases, it is recommended to start the application from a command prompt.
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
To improve stability, the application is built in `onedir` mode instead of `onefile` mode.  
Therefore, the entire `dist/LeafShapeTool/` folder must be distributed together.

Distributing only `LeafShapeTool.exe` will not work properly.
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
## About the SAM2 Module

When building from the spec file, the [Segment Anything Model 2](https://ai.meta.com/sam2/) (SAM2) [@ravi2024sam2segmentimages] module is not included by default.  
To enable SAM2, follow the steps in the [next section](@sec-enable-sam2).
:::

## Enabling the SAM2 Module {#sec-enable-sam2}

To reduce file size, build time, and dependency complexity, **the SAM2 folder is excluded** from the default build.  
However, the executable distributed on the [Releases page](https://github.com/maple60/morphometrics-tool/releases) already includes SAM2.

To enable SAM2 manually, follow the steps below.

### 1. Clone the SAM2 Repository

Clone the SAM2 repository from GitHub into the build directory:

```bash
cd dist/LeafShapeTool/
git clone https://github.com/facebookresearch/sam2.git
```

### 2. Download the SAM2 Checkpoints

The SAM2 model requires several checkpoint files.
Download them manually and place them under `sam2/checkpoints/`.

For details, refer to the following official documentation:


- [Model Description - facebookresearch/sam2](https://github.com/facebookresearch/sam2#:~:text=(state)%3A%0A%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20...-,Model%20Description,-SAM%202.1%20checkpoints)

If you are using **macOS**, **Linux**, or Windows with a **Bash** environment, you can automatically download the checkpoints with:

```bash
cd checkpoints && \
./download_ckpts.sh && \
cd ..
```

After this step, the SAM2 module will be available.

## Current Limitations

The generated executable is **quite large** because PyInstaller bundles all dependencies.  
We are considering alternative build systems such as [Nuitka](https://nuitka.net/).

However, Nuitka currently has **limited compatibility with PyQt**, and it did not work properly in our environment.

- Reference: [Nuitka PyQt5 support](https://nuitka.net/pages/pyqt5.html)

## Useful References for Build and Error Troubleshooting

- [[WinError 1114] A dynamic link library (DLL) initialization routine failed #166628 - pytorch/pytorch](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/166628)
- [How to use SAM2 if my own project already initialized Hydra? #179 - facebookresearch/sam2](https://github.com/facebookresearch/sam2/issues/179)
- [pyinstaller failed to package sam2 (segment anything model2) #8966 - pyinstaller/pyinstaller](https://github.com/pyinstaller/pyinstaller/issues/8966)

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
